issue_title: [perf] Service worker triggers a redundant full page reload on every first visit
issue_body:
Part of epic #91. Severity: **MEDIUM**.

### Location
- `assets/sw-register.js:73-78` — `controllerchange` handler
- `webpack.config.js` — GenerateSW `clientsClaim: true`

### Problem
A first-time visitor loads the page uncontrolled; `register('/sw.js')` resolves early (before install/activate) and attaches a `controllerchange` listener. The SW then activates and Workbox's `clientsClaim()` runs `clients.claim()`, which fires `controllerchange` on the just-loaded page (uncontrolled→controlled transition). The handler reloads **unconditionally** — its only guard is a re-entry flag (`reloading`), with **no first-install check**.

The sibling path `showUpdateBanner` (`sw-register.js:103-108`) *does* gate on `navigator.serviceWorker.controller` to distinguish first-install from update; the reload handler omits that exact check. This is the textbook `clientsClaim` + reload footgun.

### Impact
Every first visit (and every visit after a cache clear / new device / new browser) gets an extra full `window.location.reload()` right after SW activation — at the most perf-critical moment, the first impression. A full reload re-parses HTML, re-executes the entire JS bundle (OpenLayers is large), tears down + rebuilds the OL `Map` and all 4 `FramePool`s (~52 `ImageLayer`s), re-issues every `GetCapabilities` fetch (caps are not precached → real network round-trips), plus a visible flash/jank. Roughly doubles first-load CPU boot work.

### Suggested fix
Capture `const hadController = !!navigator.serviceWorker.controller;` at registration time; in the `controllerchange` handler `if (!hadController) return;` — reload only when replacing a *prior* controller (a genuine update). Same first-install distinction `showUpdateBanner` already makes.
